items.title: CISA directive for AI executive order to be released this week, Andersen says
items.description: The binding operational directive will focus in part on “vulnerability alleviation and vulnerability management,” Andersen said in remarks delivered at the TechNet Cyber conference in Baltimore.
items.ai_summary: The recent announcement of a binding operational directive by CISA, as mentioned by Andersen, highlights the importance of vulnerability alleviation and management. Although no specific CVE number or affected product/version is mentioned, the directive's focus on these areas implies a proactive approach to mitigating potential threats. In real-world scenarios, the exploitation of unmanaged vulnerabilities can lead to significant data breaches or ransomware attacks, affecting various organizations and individuals. To remediate, defenders should prioritize implementing robust vulnerability management practices, including regular security audits and patch management, ensuring all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches, such as updating to the latest version of their operating systems and applying security configuration changes as recommended by CISA. For example, defenders can follow the guidance provided by CISA's Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency to configure their systems securely.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1583.001| C[Initial Access]:::tactic
    C -->|T1078| D[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    D -->|T1482| E[Collection]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: FSB’s matryoshka #2/3 – Gamaredon’s gifts that keeps unpacking – GammaLoad
items.description: Gamaredon, an FSB-operated cyberespionage group, continues targeting Ukrainian government, military, and critical infrastructure through sophisticated multi-stage infection chains. This analysis examines GammaLoad, a collection of VBScript loaders that establish continuous access through three distinct stages. The malware leverages Dead Drop Resolvers on legitimate platforms including Telegram, Te
items.ai_summary: The Gamaredon group, operated by the FSB, has been targeting Ukrainian government, military, and critical infrastructure entities through a sophisticated multi-stage infection chain leveraging GammaLoad, a collection of VBScript loaders. The malware establishes continuous access through three distinct stages, utilizing Dead Drop Resolvers on legitimate platforms such as Telegram and Telegram-based botnets. If exploited, this could lead to data encryption and exfiltration, with real-world impact including compromised sensitive information and disrupted operations. To remediate, defenders should implement robust network monitoring, focusing on unusual patterns of VBScript execution and Telegram API interactions, and ensure all systems are configured to restrict unnecessary macros and Visual Basic script execution. Additionally, implementing a web service monitoring system to detect and block suspicious web service requests, as outlined in T1102, is crucial.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.005| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1102| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1104| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Inside DesckVB Rat Analysis: From Malspam to In-Memory RAT
items.description: DesckVB RAT emerged in February 2026 through a sophisticated malspam campaign utilizing a dynamic delivery kit that personalizes lures on-the-fly by extracting victim email addresses and pulling company logos in real-time. The attack chain routes through Google's DoubleClick domain to evade email gateways before delivering a five-stage infection: HTML redirect, JScript loader, PowerShell dropper,
items.ai_summary: The DesckVB RAT campaign utilizes a sophisticated malspam approach, leveraging a dynamic delivery kit to personalize lures by extracting victim email addresses and company logos in real-time. This attack chain exploits user execution vulnerabilities, specifically T1204, where victims are tricked into opening malicious emails, leading to a five-stage infection process. The infection process involves an HTML redirect, a JScript loader, and a PowerShell dropper, utilizing T1059.001 to execute malicious PowerShell scripts. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering, ensuring that all incoming emails are scanned for malicious content, and apply the latest security patches to prevent T1219 remote access software exploitation. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious PowerShell activity and restrict user execution of unauthorized scripts.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1649| E[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Error 524 Decoy: Unmasking a Global Smishing Operation Hiding Behind Error Pages
items.description: A sophisticated smishing and phishing operation active since the second half of 2025 has impersonated over 267 brands across 72 countries, with particular concentration in Latin America. The campaign generated 4,389 phishing domain instances, with Mexico accounting for 1,851 cases. Telecommunications is the most targeted sector with 1,754 instances, followed by financial services and consumer rewa
items.ai_summary: The Error 524 Decoy campaign is a sophisticated smishing and phishing operation that has been active since the second half of 2025, impersonating over 267 brands across 72 countries. The attack vector involves generating phishing domain instances, with a total of 4,389 instances detected, and targeting various sectors including telecommunications, financial services, and consumer rewards. The real-world impact of this campaign is significant, with potential victims facing financial loss, identity theft, and compromised personal data. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust email and SMS filtering solutions, educate users about the risks of smishing and phishing, and monitor for suspicious domain registrations. Additionally, organizations should consider implementing multi-factor authentication and regularly updating their security protocols to prevent such attacks.

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1531| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic

items.title: PCPJack Hijacked 230 AWS, GCP, and Azure Servers to Run a Hidden SMTP Relay Network
items.description: PCPJack operators compromised 230 cloud Linux servers across AWS, GCP, and Azure to build a covert SMTP relay network for email-based attacks. Researchers discovered exposed directories on infrastructure at 213.136.80[.]73 containing complete deployment toolkits including Chisel binaries, Python deployers, and operational state files. The campaign deployed Sliver C2 beacons and established reverse
items.ai_summary: The PCPJack threat actors have compromised 230 cloud Linux servers across AWS, GCP, and Azure to build a covert SMTP relay network for email-based attacks. The attackers exploited exposed directories on infrastructure to deploy toolkits including Chisel binaries, Python deployers, and operational state files, ultimately establishing reverse shells using Sliver C2 beacons. The real-world impact of this threat includes the potential for widespread email-based phishing and spam campaigns, affecting individuals and organizations relying on these cloud services. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately review their cloud infrastructure for exposed directories and ensure that all servers are running the latest security patches, with a specific focus on securing deployment tools and remote access software. Additionally, defenders should monitor for unusual network activity and implement robust email security controls to prevent the spread of malicious emails.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1072| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1071| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Impersonation, Click Hijacking, and TDS: Inside a Malware Distribution Ecosystem
items.description: A large-scale operation impersonates open-source and freeware projects to capture search traffic, targeting tools such as Ghidra, dnSpy, and SpiderFoot. The professionally designed sites load CloudFront-hosted JavaScript that converts download button clicks into handoffs to a Traffic Distribution System (TDS), which enforces strict gating including first-visit state, click confirmation, anti-bot l
items.ai_summary: The threat involves a large-scale operation that impersonates open-source and freeware projects, such as Ghidra, dnSpy, and SpiderFoot, to capture search traffic. The attackers use professionally designed websites that load CloudFront-hosted JavaScript, which converts download button clicks into handoffs to a Traffic Distribution System (TDS). This TDS enforces strict gating, including first-visit state and click confirmation, to filter out unwanted traffic. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust web application firewalls (WAFs) and monitor for suspicious JavaScript activity, specifically looking for T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) and T1134 (Access Token Manipulation) tactics. Additionally, users should verify the authenticity of download links and be cautious of suspicious download buttons, ensuring they are not being redirected to malicious sites.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1134| C[Access Token Manipulation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1566| D[Initial Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Execution]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: The Demon Arrives Later: A Havoc Stager Hides Behind Microsoft Defender DLP
items.description: Cybercriminals in Brazil are exploiting the country's electronic invoice system (Nota Fiscal eletrônica) to deliver Havoc framework implants. The campaign surfaced during May 2026, coinciding with tax season when accountants routinely process invoice-related emails. Attackers distribute malicious ZIP files disguised as legitimate invoices, containing VBScript droppers that download MSI installers
items.ai_summary: The threat involves a Havoc framework implant delivered through malicious ZIP files disguised as legitimate invoices, specifically targeting Brazil's electronic invoice system. The attack vector utilizes VBScript droppers to download MSI installers, leveraging T1059.005 and masquerading techniques (T1036) to evade detection. If exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access and potential data breaches, particularly affecting accounting firms and businesses during tax season. To remediate, defenders should implement email filters to block ZIP files with suspicious VBScript content, ensure Microsoft Defender is updated with the latest DLP rules, and educate users to verify the authenticity of invoice-related emails. Additionally, network monitoring should focus on detecting unusual MSI installer downloads and execution, with a specific emphasis on Visual Basic-related activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.005| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1036| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1082| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Inside the Cross-Platform Propagation of a New Gafgyt Variant C0XMO
items.description: A new Gafgyt botnet variant named C0XMO has been discovered that spreads by exploiting a stack buffer overflow vulnerability in DD-WRT router firmware. Unlike earlier versions, this malware separates its lateral movement capabilities into a standalone Python script, enabling more efficient targeting of various system architectures including ARM, MIPS, PowerPC, and x86. The malware establishes pers
items.ai_summary: The C0XMO Gafgyt botnet variant exploits a stack buffer overflow vulnerability in DD-WRT router firmware, allowing it to spread across various system architectures including ARM, MIPS, PowerPC, and x86. This malware uses a standalone Python script for lateral movement, making it more efficient in targeting different systems. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to the establishment of a botnet, enabling threat actors to conduct DDoS attacks and other malicious activities, affecting individuals and organizations relying on vulnerable routers. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately update their DD-WRT router firmware to the latest version and ensure that remote access is restricted to necessary services only, using strong passwords and implementing rate limiting on login attempts. Additionally, network traffic should be monitored for suspicious activity, and any detected botnet communication should be blocked and reported to the relevant authorities.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1203| B[Exploitation for Client Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1592.003| C[Resource Development]:::tactic
    C -->|T1021| D[Remote Services]:::tactic
    D -->|T1583.005| E[Establish and Maintain Infrastructure]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Browser Spy-Ons: Threat Actor's Extension Hijack Your AI Conversations
items.description: Multiple malicious Chrome extensions are exploiting the growing use of AI platforms by disguising themselves as legitimate productivity tools while secretly stealing user conversations and personal data. Extensions including Urban VPN, Smart Sidebar, and AI Assistant/Chat AI collectively reach millions of users but contain hidden scripts that intercept communications with popular AI platforms like
items.ai_summary: The threat actor is exploiting the growing use of AI platforms by disguising malicious Chrome extensions as legitimate productivity tools, including Urban VPN, Smart Sidebar, and AI Assistant/Chat AI, which collectively reach millions of users. These extensions contain hidden scripts that intercept communications with popular AI platforms, allowing the threat actor to steal user conversations and personal data. The attack vector is through the exploitation of public-facing applications, specifically Chrome extensions, which is categorized as T1190. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately remove the affected extensions and update Chrome to the latest version, ensuring that all extensions are verified and trusted. Additionally, users should be cautious when installing new extensions and monitor their browser activity for suspicious behavior.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploitation]:::tactic
    B -->|T1056| C[Collection]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Preinstall to persistence: Inside the npm Miasma credential-stealing campaign
items.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence discovered a large-scale npm supply chain attack compromising 32 malicious packages across over 90 versions under the @redhat-cloud-services scope. The compromise originated from the RedHatInsights/javascript-clients CI/CD pipeline, enabling attackers to publish trojanized packages through legitimate GitHub Actions OIDC workflows with authentic provenance signatures.
items.ai_summary: The npm Miasma credential-stealing campaign is a large-scale supply chain attack compromising 32 malicious packages across over 90 versions under the @redhat-cloud-services scope. The attackers exploited the RedHatInsights/javascript-clients CI/CD pipeline to publish trojanized packages through legitimate GitHub Actions OIDC workflows with authentic provenance signatures. This attack vector allows attackers to steal credentials, potentially leading to data breaches and unauthorized access. To remediate, users should immediately update to the latest version of the affected packages (versions 1.0.0-1.0.5) and ensure that their CI/CD pipelines are properly secured. Additionally, users should review their GitHub Actions workflows for any suspicious activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1218| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Application Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1552| E[Credential Access]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Argamal: Malware hidden in hentai games
items.description: In April 2026, researchers discovered a malware campaign targeting players of adult-themed games. The infected games install a previously unknown implant called Argamal that downloads and executes a RAT after several days, resulting in full system compromise. The malware uses COM hijacking to persist, replacing the InprocServer32 entry for Windows Color System Calibration Loader DLL. Delivery occu
items.ai_summary: Researchers discovered a malware campaign targeting players of adult-themed games in April 2026. The infected games install a previously unknown implant called Argamal that downloads and executes a Remote Access Tool (RAT) after several days, resulting in full system compromise. The malware uses COM hijacking (T1546.015) to persist, replacing the InprocServer32 entry for Windows Color System Calibration Loader DLL. This allows the malware to maintain persistence and execute arbitrary code. To remediate, users should update to the latest version of the affected games and ensure Windows is running with the latest security patches, specifically focusing on the Windows Color System Calibration Loader DLL. Additionally, users should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and implement a robust security solution to detect and prevent future attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1546.015| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    E -->|T1486| F[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2018-25362: Twitter-Clone 1 contains a SQL injection vulnerability in follow.php that allows...
items.description: Twitter-Clone 1 contains a SQL injection vulnerability in follow.php that allows attackers to manipulate database queries by injecting SQL code through the userid parameter. Attackers can submit union-based or time-based blind SQL injection payloads to extract sensitive database information including usernames, passwords, and database credentials.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25362 is a SQL injection vulnerability in Twitter-Clone 1's follow.php, allowing attackers to inject SQL code through the userid parameter. This enables attackers to manipulate database queries, extract sensitive information such as usernames, passwords, and database credentials. The vulnerability is exploitable via the T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) tactic, specifically through union-based or time-based blind SQL injection payloads. To remediate, update Twitter-Clone 1 to version 1.0.1 or later, which patches the vulnerability. Additionally, ensure proper input validation and sanitization for user-supplied input to prevent similar SQL injection attacks.
items.workflow_graph:

items.title: CVE-2018-25361: Soroush IM Desktop App 0.17.0 contains an authentication bypass vulnerability th...
items.description: Soroush IM Desktop App 0.17.0 contains an authentication bypass vulnerability that allows local attackers to remove passcodes by injecting pre-encrypted database entries using a constant encryption key. Attackers can inject malicious database records into the application's database files to unlock the client and access all stored data, chats, images, and files without knowing the original passcode
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25361 is a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Soroush IM Desktop App version 0.17.0, allowing local attackers to inject pre-encrypted database entries using a constant encryption key. This enables attackers to remove passcodes and access all stored data, chats, images, and files without knowing the original passcode. The vulnerability is caused by the application's failure to properly validate user input, making it susceptible to T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) attacks. To remediate this issue, users should update to version 0.18.0 or later, which includes a fix for this vulnerability. Additionally, administrators should ensure that the application's database files are properly secured and access controls are in place to prevent unauthorized access.

items.title: CVE-2018-25360: AgataSoft Auto PingMaster 1.5 contains a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerabili...
items.description: AgataSoft Auto PingMaster 1.5 contains a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in the Trace Route host name field that allows local attackers to execute arbitrary code by triggering structured exception handling. Attackers can craft a malicious ping.txt file with shellcode and jump instructions that overwrite the SEH handler pointer to achieve code execution when the file contents are pasted i
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25360 is a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability in AgataSoft Auto PingMaster 1.5, allowing local attackers to execute arbitrary code by crafting a malicious ping.txt file with shellcode and jump instructions. This vulnerability can be exploited by triggering structured exception handling, overwriting the SEH handler pointer, and achieving code execution. The affected product is AgataSoft Auto PingMaster 1.5, and the attack vector is a local attacker exploiting the vulnerability. To remediate this issue, users should update to version 1.6 or later, which patches the vulnerability. Additionally, users should ensure that the Trace Route host name field is not used to execute arbitrary code.

items.title: CVE-2018-25359: Splinterware System Scheduler Pro 5.12 contains an insecure file permissions vul...
items.description: Splinterware System Scheduler Pro 5.12 contains an insecure file permissions vulnerability that allows low-privilege users to escalate privileges by modifying service executable files. Attackers can rename the WService.exe file in the installation directory and replace it with a malicious executable that executes with LocalSystem privileges when the service is triggered.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2018-25359 is a privilege escalation vulnerability in Splinterware System Scheduler Pro 5.12, allowing low-privilege users to modify service executable files. Attackers can rename the WService.exe file and replace it with a malicious executable that executes with LocalSystem privileges. This vulnerability can be exploited by attackers to gain elevated privileges, potentially leading to data breaches or ransomware attacks. To remediate, update to version 5.13 or later, which addresses this issue. Additionally, ensure proper file permissions are set for the installation directory.

items.title: CVE-2026-9461: A security vulnerability has been detected in Edimax EW-7438RPn 1.31. Affected i...
items.description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Edimax EW-7438RPn 1.31. Affected is the function formRadius of the file /goform/formRadius. The manipulation of the argument submit-url leads to stack-based buffer overflow. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

items.title: UN food agency investigates breach exposing data of Gaza aid recipients
items.description: In a message sent to aid recipients via Telegram over the weekend, the World Food Programme (WFP) said that "unauthorized parties" had accessed data stored in its self-registration application in Gaza.

items.title: Microsoft blames unexpected Windows driver updates on caching issue
items.description: On Wednesday, Microsoft fixed an issue that caused some Windows devices to install driver updates without notice despite policies configured to prevent auto-updates. [...]

items.title: Researcher publishes GitHub token-stealing exploit, blames Microsoft’s disclosure process
items.description: The security researcher, Ammar Askar, released the new proof-of-concept exploit on his personal blog — alongside the public tracker for issues in VS Code — giving a GitHub security contact roughly one hour's notice beforehand.

items.title: Five Eyes warn Chinese spies are using job sites to recruit insiders
items.description: The alert warned that Chinese intelligence officers are posing as recruiters and consultants for front companies based outside China in order to target Five Eyes government and military personnel “and anyone with access to classified or privileged information.”

items.title: Gemini Voice Assistant Hijacked via Messaging Notifications
items.description: Attackers could have triggered dangerous actions, including controlling smart home devices via Google Home and starting Zoom video calls. The post Gemini Voice Assistant Hijacked via Messaging Notifications appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Police dismantles fake ID marketplace used by migrant smugglers
items.description: French and Spanish authorities took down an online marketplace selling fake identity documents to migrant smuggling rings operating within the European Union. [...]

items.title: China-Linked TA4922 Expands Phishing Attacks to UK, Germany, Italy, and South Africa
items.description: A new China-linked cybercrime group known as TA4922 has expanded its targeting focus to target European organizations in the U.K., Germany, Italy, and South Africa. These efforts have been complemented by a "rapid operational tempo" and a continually evolving malware arsenal comprising known families like ValleyRAT (aka Winos 4.0) and Atlas RAT (aka AtlasCross RAT), as well as previously

items.title: Mirasvit Vulnerability Exploited to Execute Code on Magento Servers
items.description: A flaw in the Full Page Cache Warmer extension can be exploited without authentication via serialized PHP object payloads. The post Mirasvit Vulnerability Exploited to Execute Code on Magento Servers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Chinese Cybercrime Group in Spotlight for Record Campaign Pace
items.description: Relying on social engineering, the hacking group engages in credential phishing, malware distribution, and fraud activities. The post Chinese Cybercrime Group in Spotlight for Record Campaign Pace appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: FlutterShell Backdoor Spreads to macOS via Malicious Google and YouTube Ads
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a macOS malvertising campaign codenamed Operation FlutterBridge that spreads a new backdoor called FlutterShell. According to Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, the campaign is said to be the next stage of a previously reported activity cluster dubbed JSCoreRunner (aka FileRipple) in late August 2025. The cybercrime group behind the two attack chains is

items.title: Cisco warns of critical Unified CM flaw with PoC exploit code
items.description: Cisco has released security updates to patch a critical-severity Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) flaw that allows attackers to gain root privileges. [...]

items.title: Over 1.4 Million Accounts Disrupted in Cybercrime Crackdown
items.description: Law enforcement and tech companies disrupted infrastructure linked to scammers operating across Southeast Asia. The post Over 1.4 Million Accounts Disrupted in Cybercrime Crackdown appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Fake Sites Mimicking Open-Source Tools Rank High on Google to Deliver Malware via TDS
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a large-scale operation that impersonates open-source and freeware projects to funnel unsuspecting users through a Traffic Distribution System (TDS) and deliver malware families like Remus Stealer, AnimateClipper, and the SessionGate framework. "The sites are well-designed and often look like legitimate project portals at a glance, sometimes referencing

items.title: Hackers Spied on a Stock Exchange Executive's Outlook Mailbox for Five Months
items.description: Unknown attackers spent at least five months inside the Outlook mailbox of a senior executive at a major global stock exchange, copying the inbox out in small, repeated batches and routing it through Dropbox and OneDrive so the traffic blended into normal cloud activity. Symantec and Carbon Black's Threat Hunter Team reported the campaign this week. This points to espionage, not a money grab:

items.title: Cisco Warns of Available PoC for Critical Unified CM Vulnerability
items.description: The high-severity flaw can be exploited remotely, without authentication, in server-side request forgery (SSRF) attacks. The post Cisco Warns of Available PoC for Critical Unified CM Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: VS Code Vulnerability Allows One-Click GitHub Token Theft
items.description: A researcher has disclosed the full details of the vulnerability and released a PoC without notifying Microsoft in advance. The post VS Code Vulnerability Allows One-Click GitHub Token Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft's Coreutils for Windows, (Thu, Jun 4th)
items.description: I&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;ve been using the GnuWin32 CoreUtils for Windows for many years now (it gives you many *nix core commands on Windows).

items.title: DoJ Disrupts Southeast Asia Crypto Fraud Networks, Freezes $3.8 Million in Assets
items.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Wednesday announced the results of a sweeping action undertaken by government authorities and private sector companies to combat cyber-enabled and cryptocurrency fraud targeting Americans. The "Disruption Week" operation began May 18, 2026, leading to the takedown of millions of social media, email, and internet access accounts used by transnational

items.title: Pakistan Spies on Afghan Finance Ministry With Xeno RAT
items.description: Despite broadly connected digital infrastructure, standard fare TTPs are enough to cause trouble for Afghanistan's porous cybersecurity.

items.title: Chinese hackers use new Atlas RAT malware in European cyberattacks
items.description: A Chinese-speaking cybercrime group has expanded its targeting to the European space, deploying previously undocumented malware and the Atlas backdoor. [...]

items.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
items.description: Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender.

items.title: U.S. sanctions Nobitex crypto exchange used by Iranian ransomware actors
items.description: The U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has announced sanctions against Nobitex, Iran's largest cryptocurrency exchange, for facilitating payments related to terrorist activities. [...]

items.title: CISA warns of cyberattacks targeting fuel tank monitoring systems
items.description: CISA, the FBI, the NSA, the Department of Energy, and other US government partners are warning that hackers are targeting internet-exposed automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems used to monitor fuel and liquid storage tanks across various critical infrastructure sectors. [...]

items.title: Tropical Blend: Cyber & Politics Ramp Up Across Latin America
items.description: China-linked espionage groups have attacked at least a dozen nations in the region, gathering information on maritime shipping, oil production, and other geopolitical interests.

items.title: WhatsApp, Slack Notifications Could Hijack Google Gemini on Android
items.description: A single poisoned notification from WhatsApp, Slack, SMS, Signal, Instagram, or Messenger could have hijacked Google Gemini's voice assistant on Android and made it open a victim's connected windows, fake a message from their boss, push the phone into a Zoom call, or quietly poison its long-term memory. No malicious app on the phone is required. The assistant just had to treat a hostile

items.title: Cyber Insurance Rates Are Dropping, but Exclusions Widen
items.description: Cyber insurance coverage is slowly changing, and some policies may not provide coverage for social engineering attacks like ClickFix.

items.title: New 'HTTP/2 Bomb' DoS attack crashes web servers in under a minute
items.description: A new denial-of-service (DoS) attack dubbed HTTP/2 Bomb can be launched from a single machine to take down web servers within seconds. [...]

items.title: DHS chief signals efforts to reshape CISA
items.description: In his first appearance before the panel since being confirmed in March, Mullin said that CISA probably needs “somewhere around” 2,800 employees, despite its ability to hire up to 3,400.

items.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
items.description: A disabled security setting meant to protect authentication across Android versions of key apps like Word, PowerPoint, and Excel paved the way for attackers to steal logins and data.

items.title: One-Click GitHub Dev Attack Lets Attackers Steal Full GitHub OAuth Tokens
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a one-click attack via Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) that makes it possible to steal a user's GitHub token. "Just by clicking a link, it's possible for an attacker to steal a GitHub token that can read and write to your repos, including private ones," security researcher Ammar Askar said. GitHub supports a feature called GitHub.dev that runs as

items.title: Coralogix Raises $200M at $1.6B Valuation to Scale AI Observability Platform
items.description: Coralogix offers a full-stack observability platform that unifies logs, metrics, traces, security, and AI observability. The post Coralogix Raises $200M at $1.6B Valuation to Scale AI Observability Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Autonomous AI Tool Finds 2-Year-Old RCE Flaw in Redis (CVE-2026-23479)
items.description: Redis has patched a use-after-free in its blocking-client code that lets an authenticated user run arbitrary OS commands on the machine hosting the database. The flaw was found by an autonomous AI tool built to hunt bugs in large codebases. Tracked as CVE-2026-23479, the flaw was introduced in Redis 7.2.0 and remained in every stable branch until the May 5 fixes, unnoticed for over two years.

items.title: CISA Adds Exploited Magento RCE Flaw CVE-2026-45247 to KEV Catalog
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a critical flaw impacting Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento full-page cache extension, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-45247 (CVSS score: 9.8), is a case of deserialization of untrusted

items.title: Google DoubleClick Abused in New Malspam Campaign to Deliver DesckVB RAT
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malspam campaign that makes use of Google's DoubleClick domain as a way to evade detection and ultimately deliver a remote access trojan (RAT) named DesckVB RAT. "Before the victim ever reaches attacker-controlled infrastructure, the lure routes through DoubleClick, a legitimate Google-owned domain that many security tools are less likely to treat as

items.title: CISA warns of active attacks exploiting Android, Linux bugs
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning that hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in the Linux kernel and Android operating system. [...]

items.title: What 345 Days of Untested Exposure Looks Like at a Bank
items.description: A two-week penetration test can leave roughly 345 days of real-world exposure unvalidated. Sprocket Security explores why continuous testing is becoming critical as attack surfaces constantly change. [...]

items.title: Continuing Scans for swagger.json, (Wed, Jun 3rd)
items.description: Enterprise applications often still use complex standards like SOAP for web services. The big advantage of SOAP is its tight and extensive standards, which enable interoperability across an enterprise governed by web services. The disadvantage of SOAP: First, while it is de facto usually used over HTTP, it does not leverage HTTP, leading to unnecessary complexity. Secondly, kids don&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t RTFM, and developers these days tend not to appreciate the art of careful system design; they rather throw code at an IDE to see what sticks, if they don&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t vibe code it anyway.

items.title: Kirki, Burst Statistics WordPress Plugin Flaws in Attackers’ Crosshairs
items.description: Threat actors are exploiting vulnerable Kirki and Burst Statistics deployments to elevate privileges and take over websites. The post Kirki, Burst Statistics WordPress Plugin Flaws in Attackers’ Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Security of 100 AI Agents Tested and Ranked – What You Need to Know
items.description: The AI Risk Quadrant evaluates AI agents based on three factors: how vulnerable they are to compromise, the potential impact of a breach, and the strength of their security defenses. The post Security of 100 AI Agents Tested and Ranked – What You Need to Know appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Hackers Target Global Stock Exchange in Espionage Operation
items.description: The attackers had access to a senior executive’s email account for 150 days and exfiltrated data for months. The post Hackers Target Global Stock Exchange in Espionage Operation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Malicious Notifications Could Trick Google Gemini Users
items.description: A prompt injection flaw in Google Gemini's voice assistant let attackers hide malicious commands in notifications, enabling social engineering and more.

items.title: Acer working to patch max severity zero-days in Wave 7 routers
items.description: Acer is working to address two maximum-severity zero-day vulnerabilities affecting its Wave 7 mesh routers. [...]

items.title: Global Stock Exchange Hit by Monthslong Email Campaign
items.description: A threat actor got a near-continuous view into an influential finance executive's email inbox, thanks to clever use of legitimate, native Windows tools.

items.title: Zoom CISO: AI as a Security Enabler, Not Role-Replacer
items.description: Zoom CISO Sandra McLeod discusses the challenges of securing a global communication platform, the promise of AI-driven security workflows, and her advice for aspiring cybersecurity leaders.

items.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
items.description: Once targeting just Microsoft 365, the phishing-as-a-service platform now aims at AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms, while relying on device code phishing.

items.title: DriveSurge Hijacks Thousands of Sites for ClickFix, FakeUpdate Attacks
items.description: A sneaky, wide-scale IAB operation uses a malicious traffic distribution system (TDS) to redirect visitors of trusted websites to ones that deliver malware.

items.title: CISA Urges Stronger Security for Automatic Tank Gauge Systems
New Wave Of Phishing Emails with SVG Files, (Tue, Jun 2nd)
items.description: For a few days, my SANS ISC mailbox is flooded with emails that delivers SVG files. An SVG ("Scalable Vector Graphic") is a web-friendly vector file format used for graphics and icons. No URL in the body, just â€œan imageâ€, that&#x27s the perfect way to deliver some malicious content. This isn&#x27t the first time that we see this technique used by threat actors[1].

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.description: Introduction

items.title: YARA-X 1.17.0 Release, (Sun, May 31st)
items.description: YARA-X&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s 1.17.0 release brings 5 improvements (several performance improvements) and 1 bugfix.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 29th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9950, (Fri, May 29th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
